headlines.description: Lionel Messi set a World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Messi bags two records as Argentina beat Austria

headlines.description: Kylian Mbappé scored twice to move into a tie for second in career World Cup goals with 16, and France played through the tournament's first rain delay to beat Iraq 3-0 on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mbappé scores twice in rain-delayed France win

headlines.description: Norway sensation Erling Haaland scored two stellar goals to lead his team to a fairly routine 3-2 win over Senegal at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland nets 2 more as Norway clinch knockouts

headlines.description: Two second-half goals from corners saw Algeria come from behind to beat Jordan 2-1
headlines.shortLinkText: Algeria come from behind to beat Jordan and bolster qualification hopes

headlines.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal rout Uzbekistan to get campaign back on track
headlines.shortLinkText: Ronaldo at the double as Portugal rout Uzbekistan

headlines.description: Thomas Tuchel's stilted side stumbled to a 0-0 draw with well-drilled Ghana as England edged closer to the World Cup knockout phase in forgettable fashion.
headlines.shortLinkText: England held by Ghana as Kane skies late chance
